sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To manage IP Access List entries, you must have [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) or [Project Network Access Manager](<Project Network Access Manager>) access
to the project.

Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves to the
project as a `Project Owner`.

sections.heading: View IP Access List Entries
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

To list IP access list entries for your project using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas accessLists list [options]

```

To return the details for the IP access list entry you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas accessLists describe <entry> [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas accessLists list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-accessLists-list) and
[atlas accessLists describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-accessLists-describe).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

| IP Address | IP address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block. If this cluster is hosted on AWS (Amazon Web Services), you can provide an AWS (Amazon Web Services) Security Group ID as well.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Comment    | Description or other information about the access list entry.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Status     | Status of the IP access list entry:StatusDescriptionInactiveAtlas is not using the IP access list entry. No cloud provider containers are provisioned for the project.PendingAtlas is configuring the IP access list entry for the project.ActiveAtlas has configured the IP access list entry for every container provisioned in the project.Active in regions: \<regions>Atlas has configured the IP access list entry for every container provisioned in the project for the regions listed, but not any other containers that exist for the project. This applies to AWS (Amazon Web Services) security groups only.FailedAtlas could not configure the IP access list entry for every container provisioned for the project. |
| Actions    | Options to Edit or Delete.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

sections.heading: Add IP Access List Entries
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

To create an IP access list for your project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas accessLists create [entry] [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas accessLists create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-accessLists-create).

**Api:**

Atlas Administration API

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [add
existing IP access list entries](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-createprojectipaccesslist).

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

- If it isn't already displayed, click the
  IP Access List tab.

- Click  Add IP Address.

> **Important:** Ensure that you add the IP address you will use to
> access MongoDB as the `admin` user.

Enter the desired IP address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing)-notated range of addresses:

| Entry                                                                 | Grants                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| An IP address                                                         | Access from that address.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| A CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing)-notated range of IP addresses | Access from the designated range of addresses.For peer VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) connections, you can specify the CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block (or a subset) or the associated Security Group.The Internet provides online tools for converting a range of IP addresses to CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing), such as http\://www\.ipaddressguide.com/cidr.IMPORTANT: Adding the CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) 0.0.0.0/0 allows access from anywhere. Ensure that strong credentials (username and password) are used for all database users when allowing access from anywhere.If you add the 0.0.0.0/0 CIDR to a project access list, Atlas sends an alert email to all users directly granted a role to the project, or indirectly granted a role through team membership (if the team is granted a role to the project). |
| Security Group ID (AWS Only)                                          | Access via Security Group membership from a peered VPC.IMPORTANT: Atlas does not support adding AWS (Amazon Web Services) security groups to IP access lists in projects with VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering connections in multiple regions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

Check the Save as temporary access list option to specify
a length of time that the IP address will be added. After this time,
Atlas removes the address from the IP access list. You can select
one of the following time periods for the address to be added:

- 6 hours

- 1 day

- 1 week

In the IP Access List view, temporary access list entries
display the time remaining until the address will expire. Once the IP
address expires and is deleted, any client or application attempting
to connect to the cluster from the address can't access the cluster.

> **Note:** You cannot set AWS (Amazon Web Services) security groups as temporary access list
> entries.

sections.heading: Modify IP Access List Entries
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

You can't modify IP access list entries with the Atlas CLI.
Select a different interface to learn how to modify IP access
list entries.

**Api:**

Atlas Administration API

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [modify
existing IP access list entries](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-createprojectipaccesslist).

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

If it isn't already displayed, click the
IP Access List tab.

Click Edit for the entry you want to modify.

You can modify the IP address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block of the entry and the
comment associated with the entry. If the entry is temporarily
added, Atlas displays the remaining time until it will
remove the entry and a dropdown to modify the duration of the
IP access list entry or convert it to a permanent entry.

> **Note:** You can't change a permanent IP access list entry to be
> temporary.

sections.heading: Delete IP Access List Entries
sections.body:
> **Important:** 
>
> When you remove an entry from the IP access list, existing connections
> from the removed addresses may remain open for a variable amount of
> time. How much time passes before Atlas closes the connection
> depends on several factors, including:
>
> - how the connection was established
>
> - how the application or [driver](drivers) using the address behaves
>
> - which protocol (like TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) or UDP (User Datagram Protocol)) the connection uses

**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

To delete an IP access list from your project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas accessLists delete <entry> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas accessLists delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-accessLists-delete).

**Api:**

Atlas Administration API

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [delete
existing users](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deleteprojectipaccesslist).

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

If it isn't already displayed, click the
IP Access List tab.
